codeReview.summary: The shared PANEL_STYLE refactor centralizes most panel chrome and tooltip colors, but it does not fully cover the requested panel set and omits the required consistency/positioning helper tests.

issues.description: The feature spec required the shared styling to apply to the Lore Log panel, but the quest log keeps a special lore tab with its own hard-coded accent colors (0xDAA520 / #DAA520) instead of using PANEL_STYLE-driven border/header treatment like the other standardized panels. This means the Lore Log surface is not actually unified under the shared config.

The feature explicitly called for tests or helper assertions covering panel style consistency and tooltip positioning logic, but the implementation adds no tests and leaves tooltip edge-handling duplicated inline across showSkillTooltip, showItemTooltip, and createTooltipContainer. Because the positioning logic is not centralized or asserted anywhere, the requested verification surface was not delivered.

sharedStateObservations.observation: UIScene now has three separate tooltip placement implementations even after introducing createTooltipContainer(); future UI workers may assume tooltip edge handling is centralized when it is not.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/scenes/UIScene.ts:1381-1469 computes skill tooltip placement inline; 2766-2865 computes item tooltip placement inline; 4322-4338 defines createTooltipContainer() but it is unused by those tooltip renderers.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The ui-worker skill says visual verification in browser is critical for UI work, but this worker transcript only records build/type/test commands and no actual interactive/browser verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ui-worker SKILL.md step 7 requires visual verification. The transcript skeleton for worker session 6c5af998-7f1b-4c56-85fd-3db171838666 shows no browser/devtools/tool-based UI checks, and the handoff.verification section lists only npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, and npx vitest run.

summary: Reviewed the ui-panel-unification commit, its handoff, and the worker transcript skeleton. The refactor improves shared panel styling, but it falls short of the requested feature because Lore Log styling remains outside the shared config and there are no tests/helper assertions for tooltip positioning or panel-style consistency.
